legal_text: Personal data shall be processed only upon obtaining consent of the Data Principal after providing notice.
plain_english_obligation: Obtain valid, unexpired, purpose-specific consent before any processing event.

signal_mapping.S1: Consent must be in active state — expired, revoked or withdrawn consent is legally invalid
signal_mapping.S2: Consent must not have lapsed before the processing timestamp — stale consent reuse is a Section 6 violation
signal_mapping.S3: Processing purpose must exactly match the purpose for which consent was obtained
signal_mapping.S4: Consent must be standalone — consent bundled with service terms is void under Section 6(2)

legal_text: Notice must specify the purpose of processing and data must only be used for that purpose.
plain_english_obligation: Data must only be used for the purpose the user was notified of and agreed to.

signal_mapping.S1: Processing purpose must not deviate from the consented purpose — any mismatch is a Section 5 violation
signal_mapping.S2: Consent version must match the current policy version — outdated consent may not cover new purposes
signal_mapping.S3: A notice specifying the processing purpose must have been provided before consent was collected

legal_text: Personal data shall be erased as soon as it is reasonable to assume that the purpose is no longer being served.
plain_english_obligation: Data must not be kept longer than necessary; delete when the purpose has been served.

signal_mapping.S1: Retention expiry date has passed — continued retention beyond this date violates Section 8(7)
signal_mapping.S2: Retention status of expired or pending_deletion with no action taken indicates a process failure
signal_mapping.S3: Absence of a legal hold means no legitimate ground exists for extending retention beyond expiry

legal_text: A notice must be given to the Data Principal describing personal data and purpose before consent.
plain_english_obligation: Users must be clearly informed about what data is collected and why before giving consent.

signal_mapping.S1: Notice must be provided before data collection — absence of notice makes subsequent consent uninformed
signal_mapping.S2: Consent channel must not be implicit or pre-ticked — these channels cannot deliver adequate notice

legal_text: Processing of personal data of children requires verifiable consent of the parent or lawful guardian.
plain_english_obligation: Children's data cannot be processed without verified guardian consent — highest protection tier.

signal_mapping.S1: Customer is flagged as a minor — Section 9 mandatory safeguards apply immediately
signal_mapping.S2: No guardian consent record is linked to this minor's account — processing is unauthorised
signal_mapping.S3: Even where a consent record exists, it must be in active state — expired guardian consent is invalid

legal_text: Consent must be obtained for each specific purpose including sharing with third parties.
plain_english_obligation: Data cannot be shared with third parties without specific consent and a valid Data Processing Agreement.

signal_mapping.S1: Data was shared with a third party — this event requires explicit consent for sharing
signal_mapping.S2: Consented purpose does not include third-party sharing — sharing exceeds the consent scope
signal_mapping.S3: No signed DPA exists for the receiving processor — violates Section 8(2) processor accountability
signal_mapping.S4: Cross-border transfer invokes additional obligations under DPDP Chapter V

legal_text: A person may process personal data only for lawful purposes and only such data as is necessary.
plain_english_obligation: Collect and access only the minimum data required for the stated purpose.

signal_mapping.S1: High or bulk data volume accessed is disproportionate to any legitimate single-event purpose
signal_mapping.S2: System stores PII categories that exceed the scope of the declared processing purpose
signal_mapping.S3: Access reason does not align with processing purpose — suggests access beyond necessity

legal_text: The Data Fiduciary shall implement reasonable security safeguards to prevent personal data breach.
plain_english_obligation: Personal data must be stored with adequate encryption and access controls.

signal_mapping.S1: PII is stored without any encryption — a direct violation of Section 8(5) minimum safeguards
signal_mapping.S2: System-level encryption is disabled — structural security gap across all data in the system
signal_mapping.S3: Encryption algorithm is below the acceptable minimum standard — CERT-In requires AES-256 minimum

legal_text: The Data Principal shall have the right to readily available means of grievance redressal.
plain_english_obligation: Organizations must provide a reachable and functional grievance mechanism for Data Principals.

signal_mapping.S1: No grievance endpoint is published — Data Principals have no means to raise complaints
signal_mapping.S2: No DPO is assigned — the accountability point for escalations is absent
signal_mapping.S3: DPO contact email is missing — even if a DPO exists, they are unreachable

legal_text: The Data Principal shall have the right to correction and erasure of personal data.
plain_english_obligation: User data must be deleted within the prescribed timeline when a valid erasure request is received.

signal_mapping.S1: An erasure request has been formally submitted by the Data Principal
signal_mapping.S2: SLA deadline for erasure fulfilment has been exceeded — this is a computable Section 12 violation
signal_mapping.S3: Data lifecycle record shows data has not reached deleted status despite an active erasure request
signal_mapping.S4: No legal hold exists — no legitimate ground exists to delay erasure

legal_text: Personal data shall be erased once the purpose is no longer being served.
plain_english_obligation: Do not keep data after its original purpose has been completed.

signal_mapping.S1: Purpose has been marked as completed — the legal basis for continued retention has expired
signal_mapping.S2: Data is still in active retention despite purpose completion — inaction is a Section 8(7) violation
signal_mapping.S3: No legal hold justifies continued retention after the purpose has been served

legal_text: Significant Data Fiduciaries must undertake measures such as audits and record keeping.
plain_english_obligation: Organizations must maintain complete audit logs and conduct audits at the required frequency.

signal_mapping.S1: Time elapsed since last audit exceeds the configured audit frequency — audit cadence is non-compliant
signal_mapping.S2: Access log records have missing outcome fields — log completeness does not meet Section 10 standards
signal_mapping.S3: No DPO assigned — oversight accountability for audit processes is absent

legal_text: Security safeguards must ensure only authorized access to personal data.
plain_english_obligation: Only employees with an authorised role and a valid business reason may access personal data.

signal_mapping.S1: Employee role is not in the authorised roles list for accessing PII — role-based access control failure
signal_mapping.S2: PII was accessed in this event — confirms that personal data was actually exposed
signal_mapping.S3: Access was denied yet the event is logged — repeated denied attempts indicate probing or misconfiguration
signal_mapping.S4: High or bulk data volume accessed by a single employee — insider threat pattern requiring investigation

legal_text: In the event of a personal data breach, the Data Fiduciary shall notify the Board and affected individuals.
plain_english_obligation: Data breaches must be reported to the Data Protection Board and affected users without undue delay.

signal_mapping.S1: A personal data breach has been detected — notification obligations under Section 8(6) are triggered
signal_mapping.S2: Notification delay exceeds 72 hours from confirmation — violates CERT-In Directions 2022 and Section 8(6)
signal_mapping.S3: Breach confirmation timestamp is missing — the notification clock cannot be verified or defended
signal_mapping.S4: Large or critical number of Data Principals affected — elevated regulatory scrutiny and penalty exposure

legal_text: The Central Government may restrict transfer of personal data to countries outside India.
plain_english_obligation: Personal data may only be transferred outside India to approved countries with adequate safeguards.

signal_mapping.S1: Cross-border flag confirms this is an international data transfer — Chapter V applies
signal_mapping.S2: Destination country is not on the approved list — transfer may be prohibited
signal_mapping.S3: No DPA governs the transfer — no contractual safeguards exist for the receiving party

legal_text: The Data Fiduciary shall be responsible for ensuring that the Data Processor processes data only as instructed and with adequate safeguards.
plain_english_obligation: Every third-party processor must have a signed, valid Data Processing Agreement before handling personal data.

signal_mapping.S1: No DPA signed — the fiduciary has no contractual basis for delegating processing to this party
signal_mapping.S2: DPA has expired — the legal framework governing this processor relationship has lapsed
signal_mapping.S3: Processor stores PII without a governing agreement — liability transfers directly to the fiduciary
